full_description: Count how many unique block colors appear, then press the number button matching that color-kind count; from left to right the buttons represent 1, 2, and 3.
schema: {A} denotes the appeared block colors, {B} denotes the matching number button, {C} denotes the color set, {a} denotes the arm used to press the button, {x} denotes the number of unique colors
preference: Mention counting unique colors, not counting blocks, the left-to-right 1/2/3 button mapping, and pressing the matching button once. Do not reveal the counted answer in subtask instructions.
seen: Count the unique colors in {A}, then press the matching button with {a}; the buttons are 1, 2, 3 from left to right.
Find how many different block colors appear and answer using the left-to-right 1/2/3 buttons.
Scan the blocks, count distinct colors, and press the matching number button.
Use {a} to press the matching button after identifying the color kinds.
unseen: Count the different block colors and press the matching number button; left to right means 1, 2, and 3.
After counting unique colors, press the corresponding button once.
Press the button whose left-to-right order equals the count of visible color kinds.

seen: scan the blocks and count unique colors
press the button matching the counted result with {a}
